item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: vetoed by author in chat

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This SCP mentions "complaining" about their experience to someone. I propose linking to SCP-2395 here, since the group behind SCP-2395 "owns" Earth, and they might be interested in any reviews.

Editing this:

"0/5 not as advertised. Will make a complaint!"

To this:

"0/5 not as advertised. Will make a complaint!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this, but wasn't feeling a whole lot until the end. It's an interesting concept, for sure, but it was just a list of where it had been…

…then, for some reason, I was charmed by the ending bit. The idea of an overeager anomalous sightseer going to different locations in history via a steel ingot is so weird, but you managed to give it a human face and made me smile. +1, this deserves to be more highly rated than it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice work! The image of 3205-A filming the battle on its phone like an asshole is really amusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I picked one I thought might be fun to watch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pleased to hear that - I try to go for that style as I'm a sucker for the 'could it actually be real???' angle of the SCP world. You can probably tell my reading involves a relatively high % of nonfiction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, I saw this and was like "Goddamn, three articles at once, better get the downvote button r— MODERNMAJORGENERAL, YOU SAY?!"

And was pleased to see the usual quality of work. This is a clever, and really impressively subtle skip. +1

Your grounded, realistic anomalies (relatively) are awesome to read, and feel a lot closer to real life than some of the more fantastical.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty good stuff! Is the E-9 mentioned here intended to be the Fire Eaters?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, nice to know it's appreciated. Maybe my 4-yearly random burst of writing motvation should get its own SCP designation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great to see you back, major. I loved your past work.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image source.

Also, the multinational travelling tank is real, for anyone who is interested in this stuff.
